resources.string: Forwards touch events from the LSFG overlay to the underlying app so you can interact with games while the frame-generation overlay is visible.
Frame generation overlay is running. Tap to stop.

WARNING — You must own Lossless Scaling on Steam.

This application requires the Lossless.dll file from Lossless Scaling by THS. You must select this file from your own legally purchased copy of Lossless Scaling on Steam.

Do NOT download Lossless.dll from third-party sources. Doing so is copyright infringement.

The developers of this application:
• do not distribute Lossless.dll
• are not affiliated with THS or Lossless Scaling
• take no responsibility for any misuse of this app

By continuing, you confirm you own a valid Lossless Scaling Steam license.
I own a Lossless Scaling license — continue
Follow these steps to get the most out of frame generation. Tap any screenshot to view it full size.
Tutorial content will be added soon. Drop your screenshots in res/drawable-nodpi and reference them in TutorialScreen.kt.

Welcome to LSFG-Android! It is strongly recommended to read the tutorial before using the app — it walks you through accessibility permissions, loading Lossless.dll, picking a target app and the overlay menu.\n\nYou can always reopen the tutorial later from the Tutorial entry on the home screen.
Choose how to open the in-game settings panel: a draggable floating icon, or a swipe-from-edge drawer.

On some devices the edge-swipe drawer may not respond, leaving you stuck on the overlay with no way to close it from inside the game. The floating icon is the recommended option. Continue with the drawer?

Hosts the overlay as a trusted accessibility surface so taps reach the app underneath on strict devices (e.g. Orange Pi 5 Ultra) where they would otherwise be blocked. Trade-off: while a session is running the overlay covers the status bar and navigation bar — you cannot pull down notifications or tap home/back. Leave OFF if your device passes taps already; turn ON only when you cannot tap through. Requires the LSFG accessibility service to be enabled.
MediaProjection captures through Android screen sharing. Shizuku and Root capture use a privileged UID-filtered target capture path, useful on devices whose MediaProjection includes overlays.
Show a live plot of generated frame intervals in the overlay. Useful for diagnosing stutter.
Number of frames produced per real frame. 2 = one interpolated frame between each pair. 3–4 recommended on mobile.
Internal motion estimation resolution. Lower = faster but less accurate.
Use the faster, lower-quality shader variant. Recommended on mid-range GPUs.
Enable only if your device and game output HDR.
Suppress generated frames during very fast camera movement to reduce missing character parts and warping. Off by default.
Runs a handcrafted NNAPI graph (unsharp-mask, detail boost, chroma clean, game crisp) entirely on the dedicated accelerator. Device: %1$s
Disabled because Android does not report a dedicated NNAPI accelerator. Device: %1$s
Strength of the enhance kernel. Higher values give a crisper image but can exaggerate compression artifacts.
Blur radius used by the unsharp-mask paths. Larger radius brings out broader edges; smaller radius targets fine detail.
1x keeps output size; 2x requests NPU bilinear upscale before the enhance pass.
Pure CPU pixel pass applied after the NPU stage. Cheap, always available, and tunable with saturation, vibrance and vignette on top of the preset.
Primary amount controlling the current CPU preset (contrast boost, sharpen amount, cinematic mix, …).
Zero desaturates the frame; 50 percent is neutral; full pushes colors toward vivid.
Lifts low-saturation pixels without over-boosting already vivid ones. Useful for grayed-out HUDs and skin tones.
Darkens the corners on a cosine falloff starting at 60% of the radius.
Use Vulkan GPU post-processing methods for image upscaling and enhancement.
Controls the requested output scale for methods that upscale.
Controls the intensity of non-scale enhancement passes.
Show a small FPS readout in the top-left of the overlay. MediaProjection counts visible capture frames; Shizuku capture counts privileged UID-filtered target frames.
Choose where the in-game drawer handle appears on the overlay.
Pick the game or app you want to apply frame generation to. It will be launched when you start the session.
Pick the apps that should automatically show the LSFG launcher when opened from the home screen. A small floating icon appears on top of the game; tap it to start the frame-generation overlay.
Enable the LSFG accessibility service so the app can detect when one of the selected games is opened.
Disable Automatic Overlay for this app
Tap to start the LSFG capture session for this app. Android will ask you to confirm screen recording.
The overlay permission is required. Please grant "Display over other apps".
The LSFG accessibility service must be enabled so touches pass through the overlay.
Screen capture was denied. LSFG cannot run without it.

On non-rooted Android, LSFG cannot be injected into another app\'s Vulkan context like on Linux. MediaProjection provides the standard capture path; Shizuku capture can use a privileged UID-filtered target path on supported builds and requires Shizuku with ADB or wireless debugging active. See the README for details.
Optional. LSFG renders at full opacity and forwards touches to the game without this service. Enable it only if you notice rendering issues on a specific device.
A report was saved. You can share it with the developer to help debug the issue. Device and extension info is already included — no personal data is attached.
No diagnostic log available yet — run a session first.
